recipe.title: Bruschetta with tomato & basil
recipe.description: Gorgeous tomato bruschetta with fresh basil and quality olive oil. There are so many delicious bruschetta toppings to choose from, but this is hard to beat.

ingredients.name: ½ a bunch of fresh basil
quality herb or white wine vinegar

steps.descr: Slice the bread 1cm-thick, then toast on a barbecue or...
steps.instructions: Slice the bread 1cm-thick, then toast on a barbecue or griddle pan.

on_enter.say: Slice the bread 1cm-thick, then toast on a barbecue or griddle pan.

steps.descr: Rub each slice lightly with a cut clove of garlic, then...
steps.instructions: Rub each slice lightly with a cut clove of garlic, then drizzle with some good extra virgin olive oil and sprinkle lightly with a little sea salt and black pepper.

on_enter.say: Rub each slice lightly with a cut clove of garlic, then drizzle with some good extra virgin olive oil and sprinkle lightly with a little sea salt and black pepper.

steps.descr: Remove the cores from the tomatoes, carefully squeeze...
steps.instructions: Remove the cores from the tomatoes, carefully squeeze out the seeds, then place in a bowl.

on_enter.say: Remove the cores from the tomatoes, carefully squeeze out the seeds, then place in a bowl.

steps.descr: Tear in the basil leaves, season with salt and pepper,...
steps.instructions: Tear in the basil leaves, season with salt and pepper, then toss with a good lug of oil and a good swig of vinegar, to balance the flavours.

on_enter.say: Tear in the basil leaves, season with salt and pepper, then toss with a good lug of oil and a good swig of vinegar, to balance the flavours.

steps.descr: Serve the tomatoes either chunky or finely chopped, or...
steps.instructions: Serve the tomatoes either chunky or finely chopped, or you can scrunch them between your fingers before putting them on your bruschetta – really tasty.

on_enter.say: Serve the tomatoes either chunky or finely chopped, or you can scrunch them between your fingers before putting them on your bruschetta – really tasty.
